content:
68Ga-PSMA PET-CT, skull base to mid-thigh

Patient: [redacted]   MRN: [redacted]   DOB: [redacted]
Study date: [redacted]   Referring clinician: [redacted]

Clinical details: Prostate adenocarcinoma (Gleason 4+3=7), treated with radical prostatectomy in [redacted] followed by salvage pelvic radiotherapy. Rising PSA, now 1.8 ng/mL (previously 0.4 ng/mL). For restaging to localise site of biochemical recurrence.

Comparison: No prior PSMA PET-CT available. Comparison made with staging CT abdomen/pelvis [redacted].

Technique: 198 MBq of 68Ga-PSMA-11 administered intravenously. Whole-body PET acquired from skull base to mid-thigh after a 60 minute uptake interval, with low-dose non-contrast CT for attenuation correction and anatomical localisation.

Findings:

Prostate bed: Post-surgical changes following radical prostatectomy. No focal abnormal tracer uptake within the prostatectomy bed to suggest local recurrence.

Lymph nodes: There is an intensely PSMA-avid left external iliac lymph node measuring 11 mm in short axis (SUVmax 9.6). Appearances are highly consistent with nodal metastatic disease. A 7 mm right common iliac node demonstrates only low-grade, equivocal tracer uptake (SUVmax 2.3) which is indeterminate and could reflect early nodal involvement or reactive change; short-interval correlation is advised. No supradiaphragmatic or retroperitoneal nodal disease.

Skeleton: There is a focal sclerotic lesion in the L3 vertebral body measuring 14 mm with corresponding intense PSMA uptake (SUVmax 7.1), in keeping with a sclerotic skeletal metastasis. Mild symmetrical tracer uptake at both sacroiliac joints corresponds to subchondral sclerosis and osteophytosis on CT and is degenerative in nature, not metastatic.

Other: Physiological tracer excretion is noted within both ureters; a focus of activity projected over the left mid-ureter follows the course of the ureter and corresponds to excreted tracer, not a pathological deposit. Physiological uptake within both kidneys, salivary glands and bowel is unremarkable. Lung bases are clear. No ascites.

Impression:
1. PSMA-avid left external iliac nodal metastasis and an avid sclerotic L3 vertebral metastasis, consistent with metastatic biochemical recurrence (M1 disease).
2. Equivocal low-grade uptake in a small right common iliac node, indeterminate; suggest short-interval follow-up.
3. No local recurrence within the prostatectomy bed.
4. Degenerative sacroiliac uptake and physiological ureteric activity, both benign.

cancer_status.progression_desc: PSMA-avid left external iliac nodal metastasis and an avid sclerotic L3 vertebral metastasis, consistent with metastatic biochemical recurrence (M1 disease).

non_cancer_findings.finding_desc: Mild symmetrical tracer uptake at both sacroiliac joints corresponds to subchondral sclerosis and osteophytosis on CT and is degenerative in nature, not metastatic.
Physiological tracer excretion within both ureters; a focus over the left mid-ureter follows the course of the ureter and corresponds to excreted tracer, not a pathological deposit.

output.report_summary: Restaging 68Ga-PSMA PET-CT for biochemical recurrence (rising PSA) following radical prostatectomy and salvage radiotherapy. Shows an intensely PSMA-avid left external iliac nodal metastasis and an avid sclerotic L3 vertebral metastasis, consistent with new metastatic disease. A small right common iliac node shows equivocal low-grade uptake and is indeterminate, with short-interval follow-up advised. No local recurrence in the prostatectomy bed. Degenerative sacroiliac joint uptake and physiological ureteric tracer activity were both judged benign and excluded from the malignant findings.
